title: 10 best herbal tinctures for chapped lips

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tinctures helps with chapped lips because of its exceptional moisturizing and soothing properties. The herb's active compounds, including flavonoids and polyphenols, work to lock in moisture and calm dryness, providing immediate relief from irritated skin. As a natural emollient, pot marigold tincture forms a protective barrier on the lips, shielding them from environmental stressors and promoting healthy hydration levels. This results in soft, supple lips that are less prone to chapping and cracking.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activity: Pot marigold's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help soothe and calm irritated lip tissue, reducing inflammation and protecting against further damage.
Emollient and moisturizing properties: The tincture's emollient properties help to lock in moisture and hydrate dry, chapped lips, while its anti-inflammatory effects reduce redness and discomfort.

Wound-healing and regenerative activity: Pot marigold's flavonoids and other compounds have been shown to stimulate collagen synthesis and accelerate wound healing, which can aid in the repair of damaged lip tissue and promote healthy skin.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: They help with chapped lips by providing antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, reducing redness and promoting wound healing.

Flavonoids (specifically, triterpene saponins and flavonoids such as rutin): They help with chapped lips by improving skin barrier function, reducing inflammation, and promoting collagen synthesis, thus aiding in the repair of damaged skin.

Triterpenoids (specifically, oleanolic acid): They help with chapped lips by exerting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which help to reduce inflammation, promote tissue repair, and soothe dry, irritated skin.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are the most used part due to their high concentration of anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help soothe and protect chapped lips.
Leaves: They are used as a secondary option due to their similar properties to flowers, providing additional benefits for lip care.
Stems: They are occasionally used for their ability to add a balancing effect to the tincture's composition, although their properties are not as prominent as those of flowers and leaves.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the flowers of calendula officinalis on a sunny day when they are in full bloom.
Clean and dry the flowers thoroughly to prevent contamination and preserve their potency.
Combine 1 part of dried flowers with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2 to 6 weeks in a cool dark place shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with chapped lips because of its soothing and calming properties. When applied topically, chamomile oil can penetrate deep into the skin to provide relief from dryness and irritation. Its anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce redness and swelling, while its antimicrobial properties prevent infection and promote healthy healing. Additionally, chamomile's natural moisturizing properties help to lock in moisture, leaving lips feeling soft, smooth, and hydrated. As a result, herbal chamomile tinctures are a popular and effective solution for alleviating chapped and dry lips.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help reduce swelling and redness associated with chapped lips.
Soothing and calming properties: The flavonoids and terpenoids in chamomile tinctures have a calming effect on the skin, providing relief from discomfort and itchiness caused by dry, chapped lips.
Antimicrobial properties: Chamomile's antimicrobial compounds help prevent infection and reduce the growth of bacteria that can exacerbate lip irritation and dryness.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid helps to reduce inflammation and soothe dry, irritated skin, thereby alleviating chapped lips.
Chamazulene: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory properties that help to calm and protect chapped lips, reducing redness and discomfort.
α-Bisabolol: This sesquiterpene has moisturizing and anti-inflammatory properties that help to hydrate and soothe chapped lips, promoting wound healing and tissue repair.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used to make tinctures for chapped lips because of their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: They are used to make tinctures for chapped lips because of their ability to calm and moisturize dry skin.
Seeds: They are used to make tinctures for chapped lips because of their emollient and protective properties, which help to lock in moisture and soothe dry skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers and clean them thoroughly for use in tincture preparation.
Combine 1 part of cleaned flowers with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place and steep the mixture for 2 to 6 weeks.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container.
Transfer the resulting tincture to dark glass bottles and store them in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with chapped lips because of its soothing and moisturizing properties. The glycyrrhizin present in licorice root has anti-inflammatory effects, which calm dry, irritated skin on the lips. Additionally, the saponins in licorice help to lock in moisture, providing long-lasting hydration and relief from lip chapping. When applied topically as a tincture, it creates a protective barrier that shields the lips from environmental stressors, promoting healthy, smooth skin. This natural remedy provides quick and effective relief for dry, cracked lips.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Licorice root contains anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin, which helps to reduce redness and swelling in chapped lips.
Emollient properties: The saponins present in licorice root act as natural emollients, helping to soften and moisturize dry, chapped skin on the lips.
Antioxidant properties: Licorice root's antioxidants help protect the lips from environmental stressors, such as wind, sun, and cold weather, which can exacerbate dryness and irritation.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: A triterpenoid saponin, glycyrrhizin helps with chapped lips by providing anti-inflammatory and moisturizing effects, soothing and protecting the skin from dryness and irritation.

Flavonoids (specifically, kaempferol and quercetin): As phenolic compounds, flavonoids contribute to the tincture's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce redness and inflammation associated with chapped lips.
Licoricidin: A phenolic compound, licoricidin has shown potential in promoting skin hydration and protecting against moisture loss, making it beneficial for soothing and repairing dry, chapped lips.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used to make tinctures for chapped lips due to their high concentration of glycyrrhizin, which provides anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Leaves: The leaves are used to make tinctures for chapped lips due to their ability to provide hydration and moisturization, helping to soothe and calm dry, chapped skin.
Barks: The barks are used to make tinctures for chapped lips due to their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation and promote healing of chapped skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 pounds of dried roots and clean them thoroughly under cold running water for 10 minutes.
Chop the cleaned roots into small pieces and soak them in 80-100 proof vodka for 2-3 weeks in a glass jar.
Strain the liquid from the jar and discard the solids by filtering through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container.
Measure out 25-30% of the total liquid volume as a final tincture strength and transfer it to a dark glass bottle with dropper.
Label the bottle with the date of preparation and dosage instructions and store it in a cool dark place for future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sunflower tinctures helps with chapped lips because of its unique combination of antioxidants, vitamins, and fatty acids. The rich content of vitamin E in sunflowers soothes and moisturizes dry, cracked skin on the lips, while the omega-6 fatty acid linoleic acid deeply nourishes and hydrates. Additionally, sunflower tinctures' anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce redness and discomfort associated with chapped lips. When applied topically, this natural blend promotes healthy lip tissue and provides long-lasting relief from dry, flaky skin.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Sunflower tincture's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals that can damage lip tissue, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy skin.
Emollient and moisturizing properties: The emollients in sunflower oil, such as fatty acids and vitamins A and E, soothe and hydrate chapped lips, locking in moisture to soften and protect the skin.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Sunflower's anti-inflammatory compounds, like flavonoids and sesquiterpenes, help reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with chapped lips, promoting a faster recovery.
remedy_constituents: Squalene: A triterpene with moisturizing properties, helping to hydrate and protect dry, chapped lips.
Phenolic acids (such as caffeic acid and ferulic acid): Antioxidant compounds that can help soothe, calm, and reduce inflammation associated with chapped lips.
Fatty acids (such as oleic acid and linoleic acid): Emollients that can help repair and lock in moisture, providing a protective barrier for dry, chapped lips.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Used for their moisturizing and emollient properties, which help to hydrate and protect dry, chapped lips.
Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, the leaves of Helianthus annuus help to soothe and calm irritated skin on the lips.
Flowers: The flowers of the sunflower plant have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce redness and swelling associated with chapped lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of helianthus annuus flowers in the morning to ensure optimal potency and freshness.
Dry the helianthus annuus flowers in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2 hours.
Combine 250ml of 95% ethanol with the dried helianthus annuus flowers in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 weeks to allow for extraction and infusion.
Strain the helianthus annuus tincture using a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort tinctures helps with chapped lips because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The herb contains compounds such as hyperforin and hypericin, which soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and swelling associated with chapped lips. Additionally, St John's Wort has antimicrobial properties that help combat bacteria and fungi that can exacerbate lip dryness and cracking. When applied topically in a tincture form, St John's Wort provides quick relief from dry, chapped lips, promoting healthy healing and hydration.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: St John's Wort contains antioxidants like hyperforin and hypericin, which help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, soothing dry and irritated lips.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and phenolic acids in St John's Wort tincture have anti-inflammatory effects, reducing redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with chapped lips.
Antimicrobial properties: Hypericin, a key component of St John's Wort, has antimicrobial properties that help combat fungal and bacterial infections that can contribute to dry, cracked lips.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds, specifically hyperoside and rutin, help alleviate dry, chapped lips by acting as antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents, reducing redness and irritation.

Naphthoquinones (Hyperforin and Hypericin): These compounds have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and protect dry, chapped lips by reducing inflammation and promoting collagen production.

Phenolic Acids (Hyperoside and Quercetin): These plant-derived acids exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to calm and heal dry, chapped lips by reducing inflammation, protecting against free radicals, and promoting tissue repair.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which provide antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties to soothe and protect chapped lips.
Flowers: They have a high content of hyperforin and hypericin, which can help to reduce inflammation, promote tissue repair, and provide antimicrobial properties to aid in lip healing.
Roots: They contain a higher concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can provide additional antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects to help soothe and protect chapped lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves in the morning when they are dry and free from dew.
Clean and dry the plant material by gently shaking off any debris and spreading it out to air dry.
Combine the plant material with a solvent such as 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking the jar occasionally.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass jar.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tinctures helps with chapped lips because of its remarkable soothing and moisturizing properties. The herbal extract contains antioxidants, flavonoids, and fatty acids that work together to provide instant relief from dryness and discomfort. As the tincture is applied topically to the affected area, it calms inflammation and irritation, reducing redness and flakiness while promoting healthy skin regeneration. Additionally, lavender's natural antibacterial properties help prevent infection, allowing for a more efficient healing process.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with chapped lips by soothing and calming the skin.
Antimicrobial properties: Lavender's antimicrobial agents inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi that can exacerbate lip dryness and chapping, promoting a healthy and balanced skin environment.
Soothing and calming effects: The sedative and anxiolytic compounds in lavender tinctures help calm and relax the lips, reducing stress and discomfort that can contribute to chapped or cracked lips.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene acts as a natural moisturizer, helping to soothe and hydrate dry, chapped lips.
Lavandulol: As a terpene, it possesses anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and irritation associated with chapped lips.
Luteolin: This flavonoid has antioxidant properties, protecting the skin from damage caused by free radicals and promoting healing in dry, cracked lip tissue.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Their soothing and moisturizing properties help to calm and heal dry, chapped lips.
Leaves: The antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties of the leaves help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in chapped lips.
Buds: The buds of Lavandula angustifolia contain oils that help to moisturize and protect dry, chapped lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25g of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers on a sunny morning after the dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm place or using a food dehydrator at 40°c for 2 hours.
Combine the dried flowers with 50% vodka as a solvent in a clean glass jar with a 1:5 ratio.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree tinctures helps with chapped lips because of its natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil extracted from the leaves of the Melaleuca alternifolia plant has been shown to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and swelling associated with chapped lips. Additionally, tea tree tincture's antifungal properties help combat fungal infections that can exacerbate dry, cracked lips, allowing for a faster recovery and smoother, healthier-looking skin. This natural remedy provides long-lasting relief from the discomfort of chapped lips.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial Properties: Tea tree oil's antimicrobial properties help to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial and fungal infections that can exacerbate dry, cracked lips.
Anti-Inflammatory Properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in tea tree oil soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and discomfort associated with chapped lips.
Antioxidant Properties: Tea tree oil's antioxidant properties help to protect the lips from environmental stressors and free radicals, which can contribute to dryness and damage.
remedy_constituents: Catechin: A phenolic compound, catechin helps with chapped lips by providing antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated skin.
Cineole: A terpene, cineole helps with chapped lips by having antimicrobial and antiseptic properties that help reduce inflammation and prevent infection.
Caryophyllene oxide: A sesquiterpene, caryophyllene oxide helps with chapped lips by reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in tea tree oil, which is known for its antimicrobial and antifungal properties that help soothe and heal chapped lips.
Buds: Contain a high concentration of tea tree oil, which can help reduce inflammation and provide relief from dry, chapped lips.
Stems: Also rich in tea tree oil, which can help protect and moisturize the skin, reducing the appearance of chapped lips.
remedy_recipe: Gather 250 grams of fresh leaves of melaleuca alternifolia and clean them thoroughly to remove dirt and debris.
Chop the leaves into small pieces and place them in a clean glass jar with a tight-fitting lid.
Fill the jar with 750 milliliters of 95 percent ethanol and seal the lid to prevent air from entering.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks and shake the jar every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tinctures helps with chapped lips because of its soothing and moisturizing properties. The herbal extract contains compounds like rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory effects that calm irritated skin. Additionally, lemon balm's natural astringent qualities help to tighten and hydrate the lips, reducing dryness and flakiness. As a result, regular use of a lemon balm tincture can provide long-lasting relief from chapped and cracked lips, leaving them soft, smooth, and healthy-looking.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lemon balm's antioxidants help to neutralize free radicals that can damage lip tissue, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in lemon balm have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to soothe and calm irritated, chapped lips.
Soothing and calming effects: Lemon balm's essential oils, such as citral and linalool, have a gentle, calming effect on the skin, providing immediate relief from dryness, itchiness, and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and swelling associated with chapped lips.
Limonene: This terpene has moisturizing and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and protect dry, chapped lips.
Apigenin: This flavonoid has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect the skin from environmental stressors and promote healthy tissue repair in chapped lips.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part of Melissa officinalis for tinctures due to their high concentration of essential oils and bioactive compounds.
Flowers: The flowers are used for their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to calm and moisturize chapped lips.
Roots: The roots are sometimes used for their gentle and non-irritating properties, which can be beneficial for sensitive skin and chapped lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of melissa officinalis leaves and flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Dry the plant material in a warm place for 7 to 10 days to reduce moisture content.
Use a ratio of 1 part melissa officinalis to 2 parts 80% ethanol to create the tincture.
Steep the mixture in a glass container for 2 to 6 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and store the tincture in a dark glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tinctures helps with chapped lips because of its soothing and moisturizing properties. The menthol in peppermint oil penetrates the skin, reducing inflammation and providing a calming sensation to dry, cracked lips. Additionally, peppermint's natural antibacterial and antifungal properties help to combat any underlying infections or irritation that may be contributing to the dryness. As a result, peppermint tinctures provide long-lasting relief from chapped lips, leaving them soft, smooth, and hydrated.

Mouth-soothing: The menthol content in peppermint tincture calms and cools the mouth, reducing irritation and pain associated with chapped lips.
Antimicrobial: Peppermint's antimicrobial properties help prevent bacterial or fungal infections that can exacerbate chapped lip conditions, promoting healing and preventing further damage.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Helps to moisturize and protect chapped lips by forming a thin layer on the skin's surface, locking in moisture and reducing dryness.
Rosmarinic acid (phenolic): Acts as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory, soothing and calming irritated skin on chapped lips, reducing redness and promoting healing.
Caryophyllene (sesquiterpene): Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, helping to reduce inflammation, prevent infection, and promote wound healing in chapped lips.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high menthol content, which helps to soothe and moisturize dry, chapped lips.
Stems: They contain menthol and other compounds that aid in reducing inflammation and providing relief from chapped lips.
Buds: They are used due to their concentrated menthol content, which helps to calm and protect dry, irritated skin on the lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the fresh leaves and flowers of mentha x piperita in the early morning after dew has evaporated.
Chop the harvested material into small pieces using sharp scissors or pruning shears to release essential oils.
Combine 1 part of the chopped material with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar to create a 1:2 ratio.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks, shaking it gently every day to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: American ginseng tinctures helps with chapped lips because of its natural anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties. Ginseng's active compounds, such as saponins and polysaccharides, work to soothe dry, irritated skin and lock in moisture, providing long-lasting relief from dryness and discomfort. Anti-inflammatory properties: The tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds can help to reduce redness and swelling associated with chapped lips, providing relief from discomfort and pain.

Wound-healing properties: American ginseng's saponins have been shown to enhance wound healing by promoting collagen synthesis, improving tissue elasticity, and reducing scar formation - all of which can be beneficial in the treatment of chapped or cracked lips.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins in Panax quinquefolius have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which could help soothe and protect dry, chapped lips from further irritation.
Terpenoids: Terpenoids in American ginseng have shown antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which could help combat infections and reduce inflammation associated with chapped lips.

Barks: The bark's flavonoids and phenolic acids have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, potentially aiding in the healing and protection of chapped lips.
Leaves: The leaves' flavonoids and sesquiterpenes may possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, helping to soothe and protect chapped lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of dried roots from mature plants in late summer or early fall.
Chop the harvested roots into small pieces using a sharp knife or a food processor.
Combine the chopped roots with 3 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks with occasional shaking.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Almond tinctures helps with chapped lips because it provides an intense dose of nourishment to the dry, cracked skin. The rich antioxidants and vitamins present in almond oil help to lock in moisture, soothing and calming irritated lip tissue. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of herbal ingredients like lavender and peppermint further reduce redness and discomfort. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh apricot kernel and calyx parts in the morning for optimal potency.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces and combine with vodka in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep for 6-8 weeks at room temperature or in a dark cupboard.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding solids.
Label and store the tincture in a cool dark place for up to two years taking 10-20 drops as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English oak tinctures helps with chapped lips because it provides a natural barrier against dryness and irritation. The herbal extract is rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and discomfort. Additionally, English oak tincture's emollient properties help to lock in moisture, leaving lips feeling soft, supple, and hydrated. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: English Oak tincture has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm chapped, dry, or irritated lips by reducing redness and swelling.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant compounds in English Oak tincture can help protect the lips from environmental stressors and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dryness and chapping.
Wound-healing: English Oak tincture has been traditionally used to promote wound healing and tissue repair, making it effective for soothing and repairing chapped or cracked lips.
remedy_parts: Roots: English Oak roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm chapped lips, reducing redness and swelling.
Barks: The barks of the English Oak tree are rich in antioxidants and astringent properties, making it effective in treating dry, cracked lips by protecting them from environmental stressors.
remedy_recipe: Harvest quercus robur leaves and stems in late summer or early fall for optimal potency.
Clean and dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator or air circulation method.
Chop the dried plant material into small pieces and combine with 60% vodka or ethanol as a solvent.
Store the mixture in a glass jar for 4-6 weeks, shaking daily, allowing the tincture to mature.
Strain the mixture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth and store the final tincture in a dark glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sumach tinctures helps with chapped lips because of its unique properties that soothe and moisturize dry, cracked skin. The herbal extract contains antioxidants and flavonoids that work to reduce inflammation and irritation, gently healing damaged lip tissue. Additionally, sumach's natural astringent properties help to constrict blood vessels, reducing redness and swelling while promoting healthy lip function. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Sumac tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gallic acid and ellagic acid, can help reduce redness and swelling associated with chapped lips.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants in sumac tincture, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, can neutralize free radicals that cause oxidative stress and dryness on the skin, making it an effective natural remedy for chapped lips.

Emollient properties: Sumac tincture's emollients, such as triglycerides and fatty acids, can help moisturize and soothe dry, cracked lips by filling in gaps between skin cells and creating a protective barrier.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sumac roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm dry, chapped lips.
Barks: The barks of the sumach plant contain astringent compounds that help reduce inflammation and promote healing of chapped lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest rhus coriaria leaves in late summer or early fall when they are dry and mature.
Chop the leaves into small pieces using scissors or a knife to ensure proper extraction.
Combine 100g of chopped rhus coriaria leaves with 500ml of vodka at room temperature for 6 weeks.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the plant material after 6 weeks.
Transfer the filtered tincture to dark glass bottles and store them in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Blackcurrant tinctures helps with chapped lips because of its unique combination of antioxidants, vitamins, and essential fatty acids. The anthocyanins present in blackcurrant extract have potent anti-inflammatory properties that soothe dry, cracked lips and provide instant relief from discomfort. Additionally, the vitamin C content helps to promote collagen production, reducing the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles around the mouth area. remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Blackcurrant tincture contains high levels of antioxidants, which help to neutralize free radicals and reduce inflammation in the skin, soothing and calming chapped lips.
Vitamin C: Blackcurrant is an excellent source of vitamin C, which plays a crucial role in the production of collagen and elastin, essential for maintaining healthy, hydrated skin on the lips.

Astringent properties: The tannins present in blackcurrant tincture have astringent properties that help to constrict blood vessels, reduce swelling, and tighten the skin, providing instant relief from dry, chapped lips.
remedy_parts: Roots: Blackcurrant roots are valued for their anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm dry, chapped lips.
Fruits: Blackcurrant fruits are rich in antioxidants and vitamins that provide hydration and nourishment to dry, cracked lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ribes nigrum berries in late summer or early fall and rinse them gently under running water.
Combine 100 grams of dried ribes nigrum berries with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture at room temperature for six to eight weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate infusion.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids and retaining the tincture.
Transfer the finished tincture to dark glass bottles, labeling them with the date and contents, and store in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Black locust tinctures helps with chapped lips because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The herbal extract contains galactose, a natural humectant that attracts and retains moisture in the skin, providing relief to dry, cracked lips. Additionally, black locust's antimicrobial compounds help to reduce inflammation and prevent infection, promoting a healthy environment for lip healing. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Black locust contains compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and redness associated with chapped lips.
Antimicrobial properties: The tannins present in black locust have antimicrobial activity, which can help combat bacterial and fungal infections that can exacerbate lip dryness and chapping.

Wound-healing properties: Black locust has been traditionally used to promote wound healing due to its high concentration of saponins, which can enhance tissue repair and accelerate the regeneration of skin cells, making it beneficial for soothing and repairing chapped lips.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help soothe and protect dry, chapped lips from infection and irritation.
Barks: With astringent properties, barks can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in chapped lips, while also providing natural protection against the elements.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh robinia pseudoacacia leaves and flowers in the early morning or late afternoon when the plant is at its peak potency.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces and combine it with 60% to 80% vodka in a clean glass jar for maceration.
Allow the mixture to steep for 4 to 6 weeks in a cool dark place, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another clean glass container and discard the plant material.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles and store it in the refrigerator at a consistent temperature of 50°f to 60°f for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage tinctures helps with chapped lips because of its exceptional moisturizing and anti-inflammatory properties. The herb's antioxidants and flavonoids work to lock in moisture, soothing dryness and cracking. Additionally, sage's antibacterial and antifungal compounds help to reduce inflammation and prevent infection. As a result, sage tinctures provide long-lasting relief from chapped lips, leaving them soft, smooth, and comfortable.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Sage tincture's antioxidants help to neutralize free radicals that can damage lip tissue, reducing inflammation and promoting healing of chapped lips.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and swelling associated with dry, chapped lips.

Emollient properties: The mucilages present in sage tincture help to lock in moisture, providing long-lasting hydration and nourishment to dry, chapped lips, promoting healing and softening of the skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, sage roots help soothe and calm dry, chapped lips.
Buds: Containing high levels of flavonoids and phenolic acids, sage buds work to protect and regenerate damaged lip tissue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm dark place for at least two weeks or until crumbly and brittle.
Combine one part dried salvia officinalis leaves with 2 parts vodka in a clean glass jar and stir well.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for six to eight weeks, shaking the jar daily, to allow infusion time.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container and discard the solids after preparation time.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder tinctures helps with chapped lips because they offer a potent blend of antioxidants, flavonoids, and essential fatty acids that work together to soothe and repair dry, chapped skin. The antiseptic properties of elderberry help to reduce inflammation and prevent infection, while the emollient properties moisturize and soften the lips. Additionally, elder tinctures can help to strengthen the natural barrier function of the skin, leaving lips feeling soft, smooth, and protected against future dryness.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant: Elder tincture's antioxidant properties help protect the lips from environmental stressors and oxidative damage, which can contribute to dryness and chapping.
Anti-inflammatory: The tincture's anti-inflammatory properties reduce redness and swelling associated with chapped lips, promoting a quicker recovery and reducing discomfort.
Moisturizing: Elder tincture's emollient and moisturizing effects help lock in hydration and soothe dry, cracked skin on the lips, providing relief from discomfort and dryness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Elder roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm chapped lips, reducing redness and discomfort.
Buds: Elder buds contain mucilage, a thick, protective substance that provides relief from dry, itchy lips and helps to lock in moisture.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10% of fresh sambucus nigra flowers in late summer and dry them completely within two days.
Grind 100 grams of dried sambucus nigra flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Combine the ground sambucus nigra powder with 400 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 6 weeks to allow for extraction and then strain the liquid through cheesecloth.
Transfer the extracted tincture into amber glass bottles and label them clearly indicating the ingredients and preparation date.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lime tinctures helps with chapped lips because they provide a potent blend of natural ingredients that soothe and moisturize dry, cracked lips. The antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties in lime tinctures help to reduce redness and irritation, while the emollients and humectants lock in moisture and promote healthy skin cell growth. As a result, herbal lime tinctures can provide long-lasting relief from chapped lips, leaving them soft, smooth, and feeling refreshed.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Herbal lime tinctures contain antimicrobial compounds like citric acid and flavonoids, which help combat bacteria and fungi that can cause lip inflammation and infection.

Moisturizing properties: The high concentration of antioxidants and fatty acids in herbal lime tinctures helps to lock in moisture and soothe dry, chapped lips by reducing water loss and providing a protective barrier.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal lime tinctures contain anti-inflammatory compounds like limonene and hesperidin, which help reduce redness and swelling associated with chapped lips, promoting healing and comfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, lime roots help soothe and calm chapped lips.
Buds: Containing flavonoids and saponins, lime buds possess natural antibacterial and antifungal properties that combat lip infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh tilia europaea flowers and leaves in early morning, ensuring at least 10% moisture content for optimal potency.
Combine 100 grams of harvested tilia europaea plant material with 400 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring complete coverage.
Store the mixture in a dark cupboard for four to six weeks, allowing for slow percolation and extraction of active compounds.
Strain the tincture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another glass container, discarding solids and preserving liquid extract.
Transfer the filtered tincture to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and label, storing in a cool place for future use at 30-40 drops per serving three times daily.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for chapped lips are concentrated plant extracts that help soothe and heal dry, cracked lips due to their moisturizing and anti-inflammatory properties. The best herbal tinctures for chapped lips include Calendula officinalis, Aloe barbadensis, Symphytum officinale, Melaleuca alternifolia, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Aloe vera, Echinacea purpurea, Mentha x piperita, Melissa officinalis, and Vitis vinifera. Without proper treatment, chapped lips can lead to painful cracks, bleeding, and even difficulty eating or speaking, negatively impacting daily life and causing discomfort, self-consciousness, and frustration.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to chapped lips that are treated with herbal tinctures are skin irritations, such as eczema, psoriasis, and minor cuts and scrapes. Herbal tinctures like calendula, aloe vera, and tea tree oil can help soothe and heal these conditions by reducing inflammation, promoting skin regeneration, and preventing infection. Additionally, tinctures made from herbs like chamomile and lavender can also calm irritated skin and promote relaxation.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with chapped lips is a blend of Calendula, Aloe Vera, and Vitamin E. Calendula tincture promotes wound healing and soothes dry skin, while Aloe Vera tincture provides intense hydration and moisturizing properties. Vitamin E tincture acts as an antioxidant, protecting the skin from damage and promoting cell regeneration. Together, these tinctures create a powerful remedy to heal and nourish chapped lips, leaving them soft, smooth, and protected. Regular use can help prevent dryness and promote long-lasting health.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Forschende Komplementarmedizin und klassische Naturheilkunde = Research in complementary and natural classical medicine, herbal tinctures are good for chapped lips due to their antibacterial properties. The study investigated the antimicrobial properties of alcohol-based herbal drug preparations, including PADMA 28, a multicompound preparation of 20 herbs, calcium sulphate, and camphor. The results showed that the ethanolic PADMA 28 tinctures exhibited an improved inhibitory effect on Gram-positive bacteria, with a minimum inhibitory concentration (MIC) of 0.38-1.51% tincture or 0.38-1.51 mg PADMA 28/ml.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis tinctures helps with chapped lips because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant's extracts soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and discomfort. Calendula's ability to lock in moisture also helps to hydrate dry, chapped lips, promoting healthy tissue repair. Regular use of Calendula tinctures can lead to softer, smoother lips, and can even help to prevent future occurrences of chapped or irritated skin. Its natural, non-greasy formula makes it a popular choice for lip care.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These help by reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, which soothes and protects chapped lips from further irritation.
Phenolic acids: These help by providing antioxidant properties that combat oxidative stress and promote skin regeneration, helping to repair and protect dry, chapped lips.
Flavonoids: These help by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that soothe and calm irritated skin, providing relief from dryness and discomfort associated with chapped lips.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Used for their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties to help heal and protect chapped lips.
Leaves: Used for their emollient and protective properties to moisturize and shield chapped lips from the environment.
Seeds: Used for their oil content, which can be extracted and used to create a nourishing and protective lip balm for chapped lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the fresh calendula flowers in the morning after the dew has dried but before the heat of the day sets in.
Dry the calendula flowers in a single layer on a paper bag in a warm dark place for 1-2 weeks.
Combine 1 part of the dried calendula flowers with 2 parts of 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place and shake it well every day for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into another glass container and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis tinctures helps with chapped lips because of its rich anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties. The tincture absorbs easily into the skin, providing quick relief from dryness and irritation. Aloe vera's soothing compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, work to calm and repair chapped lips, promoting healthy cell regeneration and hydration. This natural remedy effectively reduces lip inflammation, leaving them soft, smooth, and protected against further dryness, making it an ideal solution for chapped and dry lips.
remedy_constituents: Aloin: Helps to lock in moisture and soothe dry, irritated skin, providing relief from chapped lips.
Aloe-emodin: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, reducing redness and swelling associated with chapped lips.
Catechin: Possesses antioxidant properties, protecting the skin from damage caused by free radicals and promoting healing of dry, cracked skin on the lips.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera leaves are rich in soothing and hydrating compounds, such as aloe-emodin, which helps to calm and moisturize dry, chapped lips.

Gel (derived from Leaves): The gel extracted from aloe vera leaves contains a high concentration of vitamins, minerals, and amino acids that help to lock in moisture and reduce inflammation in dry lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 mature leaves of aloe barbadensis in the morning after the dew has dried for optimal potency.
Chop the harvested leaves into small pieces using a sharp knife or a food processor for efficient processing.
Combine the chopped aloe leaves with 80 proof vodka in a ratio of 1:2 to create the tincture base.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the container every day for even extraction.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle for storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale tinctures helps with chapped lips because of its ability to soothe and heal dry, irritated skin. The active compounds in the tincture, such as allantoin and rosmarinic acid, have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce redness and swelling. Additionally, the tincture's moisturizing effects help lock in moisture, promoting the regeneration of healthy skin cells. As a result, chapped lips can be quickly and effectively treated with Symphytum officinale tinctures, providing relief from discomfort and promoting a healthy, smooth lip environment.
remedy_constituents: Allantoin: It helps to lock in moisture and protect the skin from further damage, promoting the healing of dry, chapped lips.
Alkaloids (specifically, verbenalin and symphytine): They possess anti-inflammatory properties that reduce redness and swelling, allowing the skin to heal and regenerate more efficiently.
Tannins: They have astringent properties that help to reduce inflammation and protect the skin from bacterial infections, promoting a healthy environment for the skin to recover from chapped lips.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in allantoin, which helps to soothe and moisturize chapped lips.
Roots: Contain glycosides and saponins that aid in wound healing and skin regeneration.
Flowers: Have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and irritation in chapped lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh symphytum officinale roots and leaves in the morning after the dew has dried.
Chop the harvested material into small pieces to ensure even extraction during the maceration process.
Combine the chopped material with 2 cups of 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar to create a 1:5 ratio.
Store the jar in a cool dark place and steep for 2-3 weeks shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia tinctures helps with chapped lips because of its remarkable moisturizing and soothing properties. The tincture, derived from the leaves of the Tea Tree plant, is rich in antioxidants and essential oils that gently hydrate and calm dry, irritated skin. It creates a protective barrier to lock in moisture, helping to repair and rejuvenate chapped lips. Regular use of Melaleuca alternifolia tinctures can leave lips feeling soft, smooth, and protected from environmental stressors, providing long-lasting relief from chapped and dry lips.
remedy_constituents: Cymene: A monoterpene that helps with chapped lips by exhibiting antimicrobial properties, preventing bacterial and fungal infections that can exacerbate lip dryness and irritation.
Limonene: A monoterpene that aids in lip health by reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, which helps to soothe and repair chapped lips.
Catechins: A type of phenolic compound that has antioxidant properties, helping to protect lip tissues from oxidative stress and promote collagen synthesis for improved skin elasticity and hydration.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of antimicrobial and antiseptic properties that help soothe and protect chapped lips.
Buds: Buds are used for their anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce redness and irritation associated with chapped lips.
Stems: Stems contain compounds that aid in wound healing and have antimicrobial properties, making them useful in tinctures for soothing and protecting chapped lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of melaleuca alternifolia at the peak of potency and dry them thoroughly in a well-ventilated area.
Combine 1 part dried leaves with 2 parts high-proof vodka in a clean glass container for a total of 250ml.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks or 4-6 weeks for a stronger tincture.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another clean container discarding the solids.
Transfer the herbal tincture to dark glass dropper bottles and label them for storage and future use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tinctures helps with chapped lips because of its moisturizing and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compound, glycyrrhizin, provides a protective barrier on the skin's surface, locking in moisture and soothing irritation. This natural remedy helps to repair and hydrate dry, chapped lips, reducing discomfort and promoting healthy skin. The antiseptic properties of Glycyrrhiza glabra also prevent bacterial and fungal infections, making it an effective solution for chapped lips. Regular use can lead to smoother, softer lips.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: These compounds help to soothe and moisturize chapped lips by forming a protective barrier on the skin's surface, locking in moisture and reducing dryness.
Flavonoids: These antioxidant compounds help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in chapped lips by neutralizing free radicals and improving blood flow to the affected area.

Triterpenoid saponins (specifically Glycyrrhizin): This compound helps to reduce inflammation and irritation in chapped lips by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and promoting the production of anti-inflammatory hormones.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used for making tinctures for chapped lips due to their high content of glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Leaves: Used for making tinctures for chapped lips due to their ability to provide antioxidant and moisturizing benefits, helping to soothe and protect dry lips.
Buds: Used for making tinctures for chapped lips due to their content of flavonoids and other compounds that help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in dry, chapped skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried roots of the plant at dawn or late afternoon to ensure optimal potency and minimize moisture content.
Clean the roots thoroughly with water and a soft-bristled brush to remove dirt and debris from the plant surface.
Combine 1 part of the cleaned roots with 2 parts of high-proof vodka or glycerin in a clean glass container.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the container daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids and preserving the tincture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera tinctures helps with chapped lips because of its natural anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties. Aloe vera contains soothing compounds that calm irritated skin, reducing redness and discomfort associated with chapped lips. Its hydrating properties help to lock in moisture, providing long-lasting relief and promoting healthy lip tissue. Regular use of aloe vera tinctures can help to repair and protect the lips, leaving them smooth, soft, and protected from further dryness. This makes it an effective natural remedy for chapped lips.
remedy_constituents: Glucomannans: These complex carbohydrates help to retain moisture in the skin, reducing dryness and irritation associated with chapped lips.
Phenolic compounds: These antioxidants help to protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healing and reducing inflammation in chapped lips.
Alprogenin (aloin): This anthraquinone glycoside helps to promote cell turnover and tissue repair, reducing the appearance of chapped and cracked skin on the lips.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The most used parts of Aloe vera for tinctures are its leaves, as they contain the highest concentration of soothing and moisturizing compounds.
Gel (from Leaves): The gel extracted from Aloe vera leaves is rich in vitamins, minerals, and amino acids, making it an effective ingredient for treating chapped lips.
Aloe Vera Extract (from Leaves): Aloe vera extract is often used in lip balms and tinctures due to its ability to hydrate and protect dry, chapped lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 fresh aloe vera leaves at dawn or early morning for optimal potency retention.
Clean and peel the leaves to remove any dirt or dead skin using a vegetable brush.
Chop the aloe vera leaves into small pieces using a sharp knife or a food processor.
Combine 250g of the chopped aloe vera pieces with 500ml of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to macerate in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks or 30 days.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tinctures helps with chapped lips because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The tincture's active compounds, such as alkylamides and phenolic acids, soothe and protect the skin from environmental stressors and dryness. By promoting collagen production and improving skin elasticity, Echinacea purpurea tinctures help to reduce lip dryness and irritation, leaving them feeling smooth and hydrated. Its natural moisturizing properties also help to lock in moisture, providing long-lasting relief from chapped lips.
remedy_constituents: Alkylamides: These constituents help with chapped lips by reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, thereby soothing and calming dry, irritated skin.
Cichoric acid: This constituent helps with chapped lips by reducing swelling and improving the skin's natural barrier function, leading to smoother, more hydrated skin.
Icariin (flavonoid glycoside): This constituent helps with chapped lips by exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect and soothe dry, chapped skin.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Used due to their high content of flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties beneficial for soothing chapped lips.
Roots: Used due to their rich content of phenolic compounds, which help to protect and moisturize the skin, making them suitable for treating chapped lips.
Leaves: Used due to their ability to provide anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to heal and soothe chapped lips quickly.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh echinacea purpurea roots and flowers in the morning after the dew has dried for optimal potency.
Clean and dry the harvested echinacea purpurea plant material using paper bags or a clean cloth within hours.
Chop the dried echinacea purpurea plant material into small pieces to increase surface area for efficient extraction 500g of material.
Steep 500g of chopped echinacea purpurea plant material in 1l of 80% ethanol for 2-3 weeks in a dark place.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita tinctures helps with chapped lips because of its natural moisturizing and soothing properties. The peppermint plant's active compounds, such as menthol and menthone, help to calm and hydrate dry, chapped skin. These compounds also have anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling and discomfort associated with chapped lips. Regular application of Mentha x piperita tincture can provide long-lasting relief and promote healthy lip tissue, leaving lips feeling soft, smooth, and protected.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Helps with chapped lips by providing a cooling and soothing effect, reducing inflammation and discomfort.
Rosmarinic acid: Assists in soothing and protecting chapped lips by its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, promoting healing and reducing dryness.
Cineole: Provides a moisturizing and anti-inflammatory effect, helping to hydrate and soothe dry, chapped lips.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain high concentrations of menthol and menthone, which help to soothe and moisturize dry, chapped lips.
Stems: They also contain menthol and other compounds that provide relief and protection for chapped lips.
Buds: The buds of Mentha x piperita contain a high concentration of menthol and other essential oils that can help to calm and heal dry, chapped lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh mentha x piperita leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated for best potency.
Chop 200 grams of fresh plant material into small pieces to release its active compounds quickly.
Combine chopped plant material with 700 milliliters of 80% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the plant material in the ethanol mixture for 2 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis tinctures helps with chapped lips because of its soothing and moisturizing properties. The tincture is extracted from the flowers of the lemon balm plant, which is rich in antioxidants and flavonoids. These compounds help to calm and hydrate the skin, reducing dryness and inflammation. The anti-inflammatory properties of Melissa officinalis also aid in repairing damaged skin cells, promoting healthy lip tissue and providing relief from chapped, cracked lips. Regular use can provide long-lasting hydration and comfort.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic Acid: A phenolic compound that helps soothe and calm dry, chapped lips by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress.
Luteolin: A flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate dryness and irritation associated with chapped lips.
Menthol: A terpene that has a cooling effect, providing temporary relief from dryness, soreness, and discomfort associated with chapped lips.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and protect chapped lips.
Flowers: The flowers are used for their calming and anti-inflammatory effects, which help reduce redness and irritation associated with chapped lips.
Stems: The stems are used for their similar properties to the leaves, providing a soothing and protective effect for chapped lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis leaves and flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated when plant is in bloom.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces using scissors or a sharp knife to increase surface area.
Combine 1 part of the chopped plant material with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Vitis vinifera tinctures helps with chapped lips because it is rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds. The tannins present in the Vitis vinifera plant have been shown to soothe and protect dry, chapped skin. By forming a barrier on the skin's surface, the tannins lock in moisture and help to repair damaged skin cells, reducing the appearance of chapped lips and promoting a smoother, healthier lip texture. Regular use of Vitis vinifera tinctures can provide long-lasting relief from chapped lips.
remedy_constituents: Anthocyanins: These powerful antioxidants help protect and soothe chapped lips by neutralizing free radicals and reducing inflammation.
Quercetin: This flavonoid anti-inflammatory agent helps alleviate chapped lip discomfort by reducing swelling and promoting wound healing.
Resveratrol: This polyphenolic compound helps to lock in moisture and protect the skin, reducing dryness and chapping associated with chapped lips.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Vitis vinifera leaves are commonly used to make tinctures for chapped lips due to their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties.
Fruits: Vitis vinifera fruits, also known as grapes, are used to make tinctures for chapped lips as they contain antioxidants and moisturizing compounds that help soothe dry skin.
Buds: Vitis vinifera buds are used to make tinctures for chapped lips due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help protect and heal dry, chapped skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh or dried vitis vinifera plant material at the peak of potency.
Chop 1 part of the harvested vitis vinifera plant material into small pieces and mix with 2 parts glycerin.
Steep the chopped vitis vinifera mixture in a clean glass jar for 2-6 weeks shaking daily.
Strain the vitis vinifera mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container.
Label and store the herbal tincture in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.
